diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Subluxation of a total cataractous lens
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient reported a change in the appearance of her left pupil from white to black, which was due to the subluxation of a previously opaque cataractous lens. Slitlamp examination confirmed the presence of the opaque crystalline lens in the lower quarter of the pupillary aperture, with iridodonesis noted. There was no evidence of trauma or other known medical conditions that could cause zonular weakness. The eye also had a history of optic nerve coloboma and a total retinal detachment, but these were not directly related to the lens subluxation.
